hyperedges.label: Local package management helpers
hyperedges.rationale: Development helpers to detach, (re)install local packages on change, and load/install dependencies during package development.

hyperedges.label: dqrng-based discrete distribution generators
hyperedges.rationale: GAMLSS count-distribution random generators that draw dqrng::dqrunif uniforms and apply the matching fq* inverse-CDF; dqrunif gives better statistical properties and speed than base runif.

hyperedges.label: dqrng-based continuous Box-Cox generators
hyperedges.rationale: Box-Cox Power Exponential and Box-Cox t continuous random generators using dqrng::dqrunif inverse-CDF sampling.

nodes.label: .onLoad
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Package load hook that initializes the data.table C API so compiled C++ functions can use it; on Windows it forces a simple data.table operation to ensure initialization, and it deliberately does not fail package loading if initialization fails.

nodes.label: lookup_dt
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Designed for efficient merging/lookup on data.table objects by mapping each unique combination of key values to a direct-address row index, which is faster than standard merges on large datasets and gives controlled lookups based on pre-defined key structures.

nodes.label: is_valid_lookup_tbl
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Ensures a lookup table is structured correctly for direct-address lookups: unique key combinations, consecutive integer key values, and the expected dense number of rows based on the Cartesian product of key levels/values.

nodes.label: set_lookup_tbl_key
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Sorts key columns (prioritising 'year') and sets them as the data.table key to optimise performance of subsequent lookup operations.

nodes.label: .cklut_value_type
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Internal helper mapping an R column class to a cklut value-type string so value columns of any type can be stored.

nodes.label: .cklut_type_code
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Internal named integer vector mapping value-type strings to numeric type codes passed to the C++ writer.

nodes.label: .cklut_order_keys
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Sorts keys the way lookup_dt does (alphabetical, with 'year' first) so cklut and lookup_dt agree on dimension order.

nodes.label: cklut_build
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Writes a dense lookup table to a memory-mapped, sharded binary format so it works for tables larger than RAM, starts instantly, and avoids rebuilding/re-subsetting the lookup table on every call.

nodes.label: cklut_open
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Opens a cklut manifest into a handle; the optional warm flag eagerly loads the table into the page cache for latency-sensitive repeated use that fits in RAM.

nodes.label: print.cklut
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: S3 print method that displays the cklut handle's schema (rows, shards, keys, value names and types).

nodes.label: .cklut_rownum
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Internal helper computing 1-based dense grid row numbers for tbl (NA = no match) via the C++ index calculation.

nodes.label: cklut_lookup
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: A drop-in for lookup_dt that matches tbl rows against a cklut table's fixed key columns ('year' prioritised) and returns value columns; exclude_col is accepted only for signature compatibility and cannot drop a fixed key dimension.

nodes.label: .cklut_key_grid
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Internal helper reconstructing the full key grid in row-major CJ order so the dense table can be read back.

nodes.label: cklut_to_dt
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Reconstructs the full dense table (all key combinations plus value columns) in row-major order back into a data.table.

nodes.label: cklut_to_csv
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Exports the full cklut table to CSV via data.table::fwrite; the output round-trips back through cklut_build.

nodes.label: cklut_to_parquet
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Exports the full cklut table to Parquet via write_parquet_dt (Apache Arrow).

nodes.label: clone_dt
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Clones a data.table multiple times and binds the copies, adding an .id column to identify each iteration while retaining the original keys.

nodes.label: absorb_dt
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Copies columns from a secondary data.table into a primary one on matching keys, replacing identically named non-key columns by temporarily renaming, joining, then restoring names.

nodes.label: del_dt_rows
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Removes rows from a data.table by index/logical mask, preserving the key and reassigning the updated table back to its original variable in the given environment.

nodes.label: do_cols_dt
nodes.author: Chris Kypridemos
nodes.rationale: Performs an arithmetic or other operation across selected columns by backtick-wrapping names, joining with an operator, and optionally wrapping in a function.

nodes.label: CKutils (utility functions for R data analysis & simulation)
nodes.norm_label: ckutils (utility functions for r data analysis & simulation)

nodes.label: cklut: larger-than-RAM memory-mapped on-disk lookup
nodes.rationale: A dense lookup table is built once; queries read value columns straight off the memory-mapped file, so the table can exceed RAM and there is no per-call rebuild.

nodes.label: drop-in for lookup_dt
nodes.rationale: cklut_lookup mirrors lookup_dt's API/return so existing call sites can swap to the on-disk engine; value columns may be double/integer/logical/factor and unmatched keys return NA exactly like lookup_dt.

nodes.label: cklut: memory-mapped on-disk lookup table (new feature)
nodes.rationale: Added so a dense lookup table can be built once and queried larger-than-RAM with no per-call rebuild; validated against both lookup_dt and absorb_dt; ~1.4-3x faster warm/in-RAM, larger structural wins are larger-than-RAM and avoiding per-call setup.
nodes.norm_label: cklut: memory-mapped on-disk lookup table (new feature)

nodes.label: fd/fp/fq/fr distribution naming convention
nodes.rationale: Fast C++ wrappers are prefixed with f (fdBCPEo vs gamlss.dist's dBCPEo) and split by role: fd density, fp probability/CDF, fq quantile, fr random generation using dqrng for high-quality reproducible RNG.

nodes.label: lookup-table key validity (factor or consecutive 1-based int)
nodes.rationale: Keys must be factors OR consecutive 1-based integers so the direct-address index is valid; use is_valid_lookup_tbl() to check.
nodes.norm_label: lookup-table key validity (factor or consecutive 1-based int)

nodes.label: GitHub Actions R-CMD-check
nodes.rationale: Comprehensive R CMD check across macOS/Windows/Ubuntu and R devel/release/oldrel-1/oldrel-2 to ensure CRAN compliance and cross-platform/backward/forward compatibility; R-devel allowed to fail.

nodes.label: rchk static memory-safety analysis
nodes.rationale: Runs kalibera/rchk Docker container on the built package to catch memory leaks, unprotected values [UP], and protect bugs [PB] in C/C++; fails only on suspicious calls or [UP] (likely package code), tolerating [PB] as Rcpp false positives.

nodes.label: test-coverage (covr + Codecov)
nodes.rationale: Measures code coverage with covr, exports Cobertura XML, and uploads to Codecov for trend tracking and PR coverage diffs.

nodes.label: direct-address O(1) indexing over dense grid
nodes.rationale: When every key combination exists (dense grid) no search is needed; the row offset is computed directly from keys with integer arithmetic and read from a memory-mapped file, giving O(1) lookups with no hashing/binary search/branches.
nodes.norm_label: direct-address o(1) indexing over dense grid

nodes.label: memory-mapped on-disk payload (larger-than-RAM)
nodes.rationale: Cross-platform mmap (POSIX mmap + Windows CreateFileMapping) gives instant startup, zero parsing, and file-backed clean pages so the table can exceed RAM; only the tiny .ckmeta manifest is read at open.

nodes.label: sharded payload (≤100MB shards aligned to inner run)
nodes.rationale: Payload split into shards ≤ a configurable cap (default 100MB), with shard sizes whole multiples of the innermost run so a scan_inner range never crosses a file.
nodes.norm_label: sharded payload (≤100mb shards aligned to inner run)

nodes.label: typed value model (f64/f32/i32/i64/lgl/str)
nodes.rationale: Typed path stores fixed-width records with any of f64/f32/i32/i64/lgl/str columns (factors as int32 dictionary codes) so it is a full general replacement for lookup_dt's any-type value columns; an all-f64 table is byte-identical to the numeric format and the at_f64 fast path recovers Reader<NV> speed.

nodes.label: lazy reads vs prefetch/warm & access-pattern hints
nodes.rationale: Reads are lazy by default (only touched ~4KB pages paged in); warm/prefetch front-loads one sequential read for latency-sensitive in-RAM services, while advise_random disables readahead to keep the resident set small for larger-than-RAM sparse access.
nodes.norm_label: lazy reads vs prefetch/warm & access-pattern hints

nodes.label: R drop-in for lookup_dt (cklut_build/cklut_lookup)
nodes.rationale: Wraps the typed reader/writer so cklut replaces lookup_dt directly; returns typed NA-correct columns as a data.table/list/merged, validated to match lookup_dt and absorb_dt exactly.

nodes.label: three access regimes: random / hot / scan
nodes.rationale: Random is DRAM-latency bound (worst case, ~117ns), hot is index-arithmetic bound (~8.5ns), sequential scan is memory-bandwidth bound (~6.9ns/cell); the FastDivU64 magic multiply-shift replaced per-lookup divide/modulo to cut the hot path ~1.4x.
nodes.norm_label: three access regimes: random / hot / scan

nodes.label: cklut rownum == lookup_dt 1-based row-major index
nodes.rationale: Expanding lookup_dt's rownum arithmetic yields row_major_index+1, identical to cklut's row-major last-dim-innermost layout, so as long as dimension order matches `on` and factor categories follow factor-level order the two must agree exactly.

nodes.label: header-only INTERFACE library + ctest test_typed
nodes.rationale: cklut is a C++17 header-only INTERFACE library; dependency-free examples and the test_typed unit test build by default, while Parquet importer and benchmarks are opt-in CMake options, and Parquet needs Apache Arrow + Parquet.

hyperedges.label: Local package management helpers
hyperedges.rationale: Development helpers to detach, (re)install local packages on change, and load/install dependencies during package development.

hyperedges.label: dqrng-based discrete distribution generators
hyperedges.rationale: GAMLSS count-distribution random generators that draw dqrng::dqrunif uniforms and apply the matching fq* inverse-CDF; dqrunif gives better statistical properties and speed than base runif.

hyperedges.label: dqrng-based continuous Box-Cox generators
hyperedges.rationale: Box-Cox Power Exponential and Box-Cox t continuous random generators using dqrng::dqrunif inverse-CDF sampling.
